parsed_review.summary: CP00-224 is a descriptor-only failure-recovery slice pack (40 RP06 units, RP06.P07.M02.S13-RP06.P07.M04.S05, risk B). Full source inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms the pack-level claims are supported and no production_ready overclaim exists (manifest production_ready=false, status=implemented_pending_review, review status=pending; command-evidence production_ready=false). Coverage is data-driven and validated against the LIVE closeout plan: unit_count 40, micro-phase rows 10+25+5, and deliverable distribution {failure_recovery:18, implementation:7, hermes_evidence:4, fixture:2, test:4, security_audit:3, claude_review:2} all match across registry binding, requirements, contract projection, and plan. All 7 required public exports exist and are surfaced via index.js wildcard re-export and the validate script. Validators are substantive (not vacuous): they assert descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer-safe/no-raw-payload per row, plus no silent success, no data leak, no cross-tenant access, no rollback/retry runtime, no-write attestation drift, Claude-not-final-approver, human-escalation-required, and contract/handoff drift. freezeCp224Result hard-freezes ~40 no-write/no-real-data boundary flags. Tests bind cp224PlanPack to the live plan and dmsContract to the real contract, asserting valid===true with error surfacing and the security boundaries. CP178+ review hardening is enforced: forbidden_review_evidence covers auth_failure, stdout_0_bytes, malformed_json, fenced_json_plus_prose, tool_call_shaped_output, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_or_enterprise_trust_claim, and the schema requires all fields. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic descriptor review-packet/receipt evidence (receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write), not product runtime. Authority boundary (read-only reviewer, human final approval required, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation) holds across code, validators, manifest, and baseline. No TODO/FIXME/stub markers in scope; no-leak guards present on all permission/audit/hermes/fixture/lock rows. The post-review artifacts (raw-output, receipt, claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection) are correctly absent for the pre-review state. No P0/P1/P2/P3 defects identified. Note: actual command execution results are taken from command-evidence.json (all exit_code 0) since execution is out of scope for a read-only review; static inspection of the traced assertions is consistent with those passing. This verdict is advisory; human final approval remains required for the enterprise trust claim.
